name: Chicken Chunks with Peanuts in Spicy Sauce (Kung Pao)
recipeIngredient: 2 Whole chicken breasts at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
Preparation: Trim ends off green onions and cut into 1-inch sections. Mix all other sauce ingredients in a 2-quart saucepan. Reserve.
Pull the skin off chicken breasts, then pull the meat from bones. Slice the meat into 1-inch strips, then crosswise to make 1-inch chunks. In a bowl large enough to hold the chicken, add egg white to water chestnut flour. Beat the mixture with a single chopstick (not an egg beater or whisk). Stir the chicken pieces into the egg mixture to coat thoroughly. Marinate for 5 minutes. Note: Water chestnut flour gives a lighter crust than cornstarch, though the latter may be substituted.
Deep-frying: Heat cooking oil in a wok or deep fryer to medium heat (you'll need more oil for deep fryer). Fry peanuts until they are a light tan color; if a test peanut browns quickly, turn down the heat. Remove peanuts with a strainer or slotted spoon; drain on paper towel or paper bag. Reserve.
Turn up the heat slightly for chicken. Test a chunk first: Chicken should raise to the surface immediately and brown in about 2 minutes. Deep fry coated chicken chunks until golden brown. Deep fry no more than 8 chunks at a time. Use long chopsticks or a spatula to keep pieces separate while they are frying. Remove with long chopsticks or slotted spoon. Reserve.
Sauce: While deep-frying chicken, heat the sauce to simmer. Add green onions and peanuts about a minute before serving. At the last minute, add chicken pieces to the sauce, mix quickly, and serve.
